definition.meaning: Above, over, higher than; upon, on top of; watching over, superior to
definition.origin: From Old English bufan, from Proto-West Germanic *biobanā, from *bi "by" + *ufanā "from above"
definition.etymology: Proto-Germanic *bi + *ufanē "from above". Cognate with Dutch boven.

definition.meaning: Apheretic form of "above"; used as adverb and preposition meaning over, higher than
definition.origin: Inherited from Germanic via Old English bufan
From Latin bos, genitive bovis "ox, cattle"
definition.etymology: Latin bos from Greek bous (βοῦς) "ox, cattle"
definition.usage: Occupational name for a plowman or herdsman; nickname for someone resembling an ox (patient, strong)

definition.origin: Cognate with English bove/above from same Proto-Germanic root

definition.meaning: (a) Above, over, higher than; (b) upon, on top of, on the surface of; (c) watching over, caring for; superior to, more exalted than
definition.source: Middle English Dictionary, University of Michigan
